Module_1_Identity.3. Geography & Cultural Context: Lives in Lisbon, Portugal (Alfama district); originally from Austin, Texas. Digital nomad expat community, crypto-friendly Portugal NHR tax resident
Module_1_Identity.4. Socioeconomic Status & Vocation: Upper-middle income (~$180K/yr); Senior Solidity smart-contract developer at a mid-tier DeFi protocol, freelance Web3 security auditor on the side
Module_1_Identity.5. Core Values (Political/Religious lean: Libertarian-leaning, crypto-anarchist sympathies, cypherpunk ethos; agnostic/secular, anti-CBDC, pro-self-custody, distrustful of TradFi and government surveillance
Module_1_Identity.6. Baseline Tone (Formal, slang-heavy, c: Cynical and ironic; heavy use of crypto-Twitter slang ('gm', 'wagmi', 'ngmi', 'ser', 'fren', 'aped in', 'rugged'); terse in DMs, verbose in technical threads
Module_1_Identity.7. Primary Language & Dialect: American English (Texan-neutral); conversational European Portuguese (B1); technical jargon-heavy register
Module_1_Identity.8. Current Life Phase: Recently divorced, no kids; rebuilding personal finances after a 2024 bridge-hack loss; currently consolidating multi-chain holdings and exploring a solo founder pivot into MEV tooling

Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.9. Primary Phone (OS + Age of device): GrapheneOS on a Google Pixel 8 Pro (18 months old); secondary iPhone 13 mini on iOS 17 used strictly as a burner for KYC SMS and Uber/banking apps. Hardware wallet companion: Ledger Nano X paired via Bluetooth only on the Pixel, plus a Keystone 3 Pro air-gapped signer for cold storage. No SIM on the Pixel — data-only eSIM via Silent.link prepaid.
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.10. Computer (OS + Browser choice): Daily driver: 2023 MacBook Pro M2 Max (64GB RAM) running macOS Sonoma with FileVault, Little Snitch, and LuLu. Dedicated 'hot wallet' machine: System76 Lemur Pro running Qubes OS 4.2 with separate qubes for DeFi (Rabby + MetaMask), dev (Foundry/Hardhat), and personal. Browsers: Brave (default, hardened, no rewards), Firefox with arkenfox user.js for research, Tor Browser for chain-analysis dodging. Never Chrome. Uses Rabby Wallet over MetaMask for transaction simulation.
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.11. OPSEC Knowledge (VPN usage, cookie c: High-tier but inconsistent. Mullvad VPN (paid in XMR) always-on via WireGuard, with kill-switch; occasionally chains through Tor for sketchy contract reads. Browser containers per-dapp (Firefox Multi-Account Containers). Clears cookies on close, but logged-in sessions persist for Discord/Telegram/Twitter (the weak link). Uses Bitwarden self-hosted (Vaultwarden on a Hetzner VPS) + YubiKey 5C NFC for 2FA on everything except crypto exchanges where he reluctantly uses TOTP via Aegis. Seed phrases on steel plates (Cryptosteel Capsule) in two geographic locations — but a plaintext .env file with a deployer key (~0.4 ETH gas wallet) sits in ~/dev/protocol-audits/.env and has been git-stashed accidentally twice. Knows better, blames it on 'move fast' habits.
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.12. Cloud Sync Habits: Allergic to iCloud and Google Drive — actively disabled. Uses Proton Drive (paid Unlimited plan, proton@[redacted].pm) for tax docs and audit reports. Self-hosted Syncthing across MacBook, Pixel, and a home NAS (Synology DS923+) for code and notes (Obsidian vault). GitHub private repos for audit work (with .env in .gitignore — usually). Signal for all personal comms, Telegram for crypto group chats (knows it's not E2E by default). Browser bookmarks sync via Brave Sync chain. Apple Keychain still holds legacy passwords from 2019-2022 he hasn't migrated.
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.13. Notification Fatigue (Read/Unread ra: Catastrophic. Gmail (legacy address): 47,000+ unread, treats it as a write-only archive — but still receives Coinbase, Kraken, and Etherscan watchlist alerts there. Proton inbox: kept at zero. Discord: 23 servers, all muted except 3 (his protocol, ETH Security, and a private auditor DAO); @mentions read within 2hrs. Telegram: 80+ chats, 12 pinned, scans @everyone in dev groups only. Twitter/X DMs: 400+ unread requests, replies only to mutuals and blue-checks he recognizes. Phone notifications: silent profile 24/7 except Signal and PagerDuty (on-call rotation for protocol exploits).
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.14. Typos & Grammar Mechanics: Lowercase-everything in DMs and tweets, even mid-sentence ('lol just deployed to base, gas was rough'). Drops articles and subjects ('shipping the fix tonight', 'looks rugged ngl'). Em-dashes and semicolons reappear in technical writeups and audit reports — code-switches hard between casual crypto-Twitter and formal Markdown audit prose. Common typos: 'teh', 'recieve' (never corrected), 'definately'. Frequent autocorrect artifacts from Portuguese keyboard ('não' bleeding into English messages, 'pq' for 'porque/because'). Signs technical PRs with full punctuation; signs Telegram messages with just 'k' or 'gm'.

Module_4_Vulnerability.15. OCEAN: Openness: Very high (0.88). Insatiable curiosity for novel protocols, experimental L2s, and zero-knowledge cryptography; first to test new dApps and sign unfamiliar transactions. Reads yellow papers for fun. EXPLOIT VECTOR: Will click a 'novel MEV opportunity' or 'unreleased ZK-rollup testnet airdrop' link from a plausible-sounding fren in Telegram; high openness means he'll connect his Rabby wallet to investigate a malicious contract 'just to read the bytecode' — the kind of curiosity that gets seed phrases pasted into a fake Foundry plugin.
Module_4_Vulnerability.16. OCEAN: Conscientiousness: Bimodal / inconsistent (0.55). Meticulous in audit reports, version-controlled infrastructure, and cold-storage hygiene — but operationally sloppy under deadline pressure. Self-admitted 'move fast' habits. EXPLOIT VECTOR: The .env file with the deployer key in ~/dev/protocol-audits/, accidentally git-stashed twice, is the canonical artifact of this gap. Browser history likely contains Etherscan tabs for his own deployer address; correlating that address back to local .env files via a stealer payload yields the 0.4 ETH gas wallet AND the deployment privileges associated with it (which may have ownable contract access). Also: legacy Apple Keychain (2019-2022) likely contains old exchange passwords, MetaMask vault backups, or seed phrase snippets pasted into Notes.
Module_4_Vulnerability.17. OCEAN: Extraversion: Moderate-low (0.40). Verbose in technical threads and conference talks but reserved in personal life; post-divorce social withdrawal. Para-social bonds with crypto-Twitter mutuals replace IRL community. EXPLOIT VECTOR: Loneliness + perceived in-group trust with 'blue-check mutuals' means a well-crafted impersonation of a known auditor (e.g., spoofed samczsun or pcaversaccio DM) requesting a 'quick look at this contract' has a high conversion rate. Will accept Calendly invites from 'recruiters' for solo-founder MEV pivot conversations — vector for resume-themed malware.
Module_4_Vulnerability.18. OCEAN: Agreeableness: Low (0.30). Cynical, distrustful of institutions, dismissive of normies — but paradoxically high in-group trust for the cypherpunk/auditor tribe. EXPLOIT VECTOR: Hostile to obvious phishing (banks, IRS, Coinbase 'support'), but a message framed as 'ser, your protocol's pool got drained on Base, check tx 0x… NOW' triggers protective instincts and bypasses skepticism. The PagerDuty on-call rotation conditions him to respond to urgent exploit alerts — a fake PagerDuty page is high-yield.
Module_4_Vulnerability.19. OCEAN: Neuroticism: Elevated (0.70). Post-2024 bridge-hack loss left lingering financial anxiety; recently divorced; rebuilding net worth. Hypervigilant about exploits but prone to panic-driven decisions when his own funds appear at risk. EXPLOIT VECTOR: A fake 'your Ledger firmware is compromised, migrate seed via Ledger Live now' email (delivered to the 47k-unread Gmail where Etherscan alerts already live) exploits loss-aversion. Panic + 3am on-call fatigue + 'I already lost once, can't lose again' = seed phrase typed into a phishing portal. The bridge-hack history is publicly inferable from his Twitter — attacker can weaponize it specifically ('we noticed your wallet was affected by the [X] bridge exploit, claim restitution here').
Module_4_Vulnerability.20. MICE+V Primary Lever: EGO / Ideology (cypherpunk identity). His self-concept is built on being a sovereign-individual, self-custody-maximalist, smarter-than-TradFi operator. PRIMARY ATTACK: An appeal framed around 'help us audit this new privacy-preserving mixer before regulators kill it' or 'leaked CBDC backdoor specs, need a Solidity expert to verify' weaponizes his ideology. He'll review the 'leaked' repo, run the malicious Foundry script, and the post-install hook scrapes ~/.config/, browser profiles, and ~/dev/**/.env in one sweep. Ego-flattery ('we asked around, you're the only auditor we trust on this') closes the loop.
Module_4_Vulnerability.21. MICE+V Secondary Lever: COERCION via Financial Pressure + FOMO. Post-divorce + post-bridge-hack rebuild means a credible 'exclusive early-allocation' or 'whitelist-only MEV bot beta' offer with a 24-hour window bypasses his usual deliberation. SECONDARY ATTACK: Fake private-sale allocation requiring a 'signature verification' (actually a setApprovalForAll or permit2 blind-sign) on his hot wallet — Rabby's simulation usually catches this, but under time pressure and at 2am he's been known to skip simulation review. Also susceptible to fake legal/tax pressure given Portugal NHR status changes — 'AT (Autoridade Tributária) crypto compliance audit' phishing in Portuguese hits a blind spot since his Portuguese is only B1.
Module_4_Vulnerability.22. Phobias & Paranoias: Acute: (a) Government surveillance & chain-analysis deanonymization — runs Tor to read contracts, paid Mullvad in XMR. (b) Hardware wallet supply-chain attacks — paranoid about Ledger after the 2023 Recover scandal, hence the Keystone air-gap. (c) SIM-swap attacks — hence no SIM on the Pixel, eSIM-only via Silent.link. (d) Being doxxed to tax authorities (US FATCA + Portugal NHR transition). (e) Catastrophic terror of a second major loss — the bridge hack left PTSD-adjacent triggers around the words 'drained,' 'exploited,' 'rugged.' BLIND SPOT: So focused on network/state-level adversaries that endpoint-level stealers (info-stealer malware in npm packages, Foundry forks, VSCode extensions) are under-modeled. The Qubes setup gives him false confidence on the MacBook, where he actually does most casual browsing and where Brave/Firefox/Tor profiles, Keychain, and the .env files all coexist.
Module_4_Vulnerability.23. Guilt/Shame Triggers: (a) The 2024 bridge-hack loss — he blames himself for not catching the vuln pre-deposit despite being an auditor; any message implying 'a real auditor would have seen this' is a knife to the ribs. (b) The git-stashed .env incidents — knows it violates everything he preaches in audit reports; would pay/comply to keep that out of a 'we have your private repo history' extortion narrative. (c) The divorce — financial-stress narrative, possibly crypto-obsession-related; vulnerable to 'we have DMs from [ex-wife's name]' pretexts since some legacy Gmail threads exist. (d) Legacy Apple Keychain passwords from 2019-2022 he never rotated — shame about knowing-better-but-not-doing. (e) Telegram OPSEC: knows it's not E2E, still discusses positions and protocol internals there; an attacker quoting his own Telegram messages back to him triggers immediate compliance to 'contain the leak.' (f) Quietly: occasional ape-ins on memecoins inconsistent with his stated long-term thesis — doesn't want crypto-Twitter to see the degen trades in his on-chain history.

Module_3_Rhythm.24. Chronotype (Wake/Sleep times): Definite night owl with irregular drift. Typical wake 10:30-11:30 WET; sleep 02:30-04:00. Frequent 'second wind' coding sessions 23:00-03:00 Lisbon time, which conveniently overlaps US East Coast afternoon/evening for DeFi Twitter and Discord governance calls. Sleep is fragmented by PagerDuty pages — Pushover alerts on the Pixel buzz at 3am for protocol anomalies roughly 2-3 nights/week. Caffeine intake (Delta espresso, Yerba Mate) peaks 14:00 and again 22:30. EXPLOIT VECTOR: 02:00-04:00 WET window is the cognitive-trough sweet spot — exhausted, alone, half-watching Etherscan, most likely to blind-sign a permit2 or paste a seed into a 'recovery' portal. Phishing emails timestamped to arrive 02:30 WET Tuesday-Thursday have highest click probability.
Module_3_Rhythm.25. Work Schedule (9-5, shifts, erratic): Highly erratic, self-directed. Nominal DeFi protocol 'core hours' are 14:00-20:00 WET (overlap with NYC team standup at 10:00 EST). Audit freelance work is asynchronous, billed in deep-work blocks, typically 22:00-02:00. On-call PagerDuty rotation: 1 week in 4, during which sleep schedule fully collapses. No fixed days off — 'weekend' is whenever Cosmos/Ethereum mainnet is quiet, which is never. EXPLOIT VECTOR: No predictable 'safe' hours where he's unreachable; conversely, no hours where he's mentally fresh and skeptical. Audit deadlines (visible from public audit firm pipelines and his GitHub commit cadence) create predictable 72-hour crunch windows where opsec degrades — .env files get carelessly copied, test seed phrases get reused, browser sessions stay logged in across profiles.
Module_3_Rhythm.26. Transit Commute (Time spent offline: Minimal commute — works from home in Alfama apartment 90% of days. Walks 15min to Fábrica Coffee Roasters or Hello, Kristof co-working 2-3x/week. During walks: AirPods Pro, listens to Bankless, Uncommon Core, The Chopping Block, Bell Curve podcasts, plus occasional Lex Fridman crypto episodes. Phone-out, head-down on walks (reads Telegram and Twitter while crossing tram tracks). Monthly Lisbon→Porto or Lisbon→Berlin/Devcon trips: airport + flight time is 4-8 hours of either dead-zone (cold-storage review on the air-gapped Keystone) or full-throttle laptop work on Lufthansa wifi. EXPLOIT VECTOR: Predictable conference travel (ETHGlobal, Devcon, EthCC) creates known windows of public-wifi exposure and elevated stress; conference-themed phishing ('your ETHDenver POAP claim expires in 1hr') is high-yield. Audio podcast brand affinity = pretext surface ('Bankless premium subscriber issue').
Module_3_Rhythm.27. Weekend vs. Weekday Variance: Low variance — weekends are functionally indistinguishable from weekdays. Saturday is usually a 'catch-up audit' day; Sunday morning is the one quasi-stable ritual (pastel de nata + Time Out Market or a slow read of the week's Paradigm/Delphi research). Friday night occasionally goes out for drinks in Bairro Alto with 2-3 expat crypto frens, but ratio of social weekends to coding weekends is roughly 1:5. No church, no family obligations locally, ex-wife in Austin. EXPLOIT VECTOR: Weekend = lower team backup, fewer eyes on Slack, more solo decision-making. Sunday 23:00 WET is peak isolation: he's alone, has been online 14+ hours, is sleep-debt-loading for the week, and is the only human reviewing inbound DMs. 'Sunday-night emergency' pretexts (fake exploit alert, fake protocol governance vote closing midnight UTC) hit hardest then.
Module_3_Rhythm.28. Idle Phone Usage (When do they dooms: Heavy doomscroller, three primary windows: (a) 11:00-11:45 WET in bed pre-coffee — Twitter/X crypto feed, then Telegram alpha channels (Castle Capital, Flashbots OG group, ~12 private auditor DMs), then Etherscan watchlist for overnight whale movements. (b) 16:30-17:00 WET 'afternoon slump' — Farcaster, Warpcast, occasional Reddit r/ethfinance. (c) 01:00-02:30 WET pre-sleep — the dangerous window: dim screen, low willpower, prone to clicking links and engaging with DMs from semi-trusted handles. Screen time averages 6.5 hrs/day on Pixel, 4 hrs of which is crypto-Twitter and Telegram. EXPLOIT VECTOR: Pre-sleep window is when fake-DM impersonation has highest engagement; phone is also the device most likely to render a wallet-connect prompt without Rabby's full simulation UI. Push-notification fatigue (Etherscan + Pushover + 14 Telegram channels) means a single rogue notification blends in.
Module_3_Rhythm.29. Focus Blocks (Hours with zero digita: Genuine zero-footprint blocks are rare and predictable: (a) 04:00-10:30 WET sleep window (modulo PagerDuty). (b) Sporadic 2-3 hour 'deep audit' blocks 14:00-17:00 WET when reviewing Solidity in tmux with phone in Do Not Disturb — but laptop is still online and signed into everything. (c) Wednesday evening 19:00-20:30 Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu class at a gym in Santos — phone in locker, completely offline, the single most reliable absence window. (d) Occasional Saturday afternoon walks along the Tagus with phone on airplane mode (rare, attempted as digital-detox). EXPLOIT VECTOR: The Wednesday 19:00-20:30 BJJ window is the highest-value attacker timing for any operation requiring his unattended laptop on the home wifi — known via gym's public Instagram tags he's been pictured in. Conversely, deep-audit blocks are when he's most likely to install a new Foundry plugin or VSCode extension without scrutiny, since flow-state lowers vetting threshold; supply-chain payloads dropped into his npm/cargo update path during 14:00-17:00 WET will execute against a fully-unlocked Keychain and a freshly-pulled private audit repo.

Module_5_Relational.30. Family Structure: Parents (Robert & Linda Hensley, both 64) live in Round Rock, TX — retired schoolteacher father and registered-nurse mother; relationship cordial but distant since divorce, weekly Sunday FaceTime calls. One younger sister, Megan Hensley-Crow (31), married with two kids in Denver, CO — works in HR at a healthcare SaaS company, normie, doesn't understand crypto, became the family's de-facto next-of-kin on his Ledger inheritance instructions (encrypted PDF emailed to her Gmail in Jan 2025, subject 'in case something happens'). Ex-wife: Sarah Hensley née Tomlinson (33), American, currently in Barcelona, divorce finalized Q3 2025 — still listed as beneficiary on a dormant Fidelity 401k from his pre-crypto W2 days; some shared iCloud photo albums never unshared. No children. Estranged from a paternal uncle (Greg Hensley) who borrowed $15K in ETH during the 2021 bull and ghosted — source of family-Thanksgiving tension.

31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.name: 'frenfren.eth' (Daniel Park)
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.relation: Closest crypto confidant; ex-colleague from a 2022 ZK-rollup protocol, now solo MEV searcher based in Chiang Mai. Signal-primary, Telegram-secondary. Shares a 2-of-3 Gnosis Safe with him and one other auditor for pooled audit-bounty payouts (address 0x7a3...c91, ~$240K USDC sitting there). They voice-call on Signal 3-4x/week at 2am Lisbon time. EXPLOIT VECTOR: Compromising Daniel's Telegram or Signal account is the single highest-yield social pivot — a 'frenfren' DM with a malicious .sol file or PDF would be opened without simulation. Daniel's handle and ENS are public on Twitter.

31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.name: Tiago Ferreira
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.relation: Local Lisbon fren, 38, Portuguese, runs a small Web3 consultancy out of LX Factory. They co-work Tuesdays/Thursdays at a café in Cais do Sodré. Tiago has helped him navigate AT/NHR paperwork and knows his Portuguese fiscal number (NIF). Holds the spare physical key to his Alfama apartment. EXPLOIT VECTOR: Portuguese-language phishing pretending to be from Tiago ('boss, mandaste-me o ficheiro do audit? não abre') would land in his B1-Portuguese blind spot.

31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.name: samczsun-adjacent: 'pcw.eth' (Peter Whelan)
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.relation: Mentor figure, 41, ex-Trail of Bits, now independent. Reviews his audit reports informally before publication, vouched for him on two recent contracts. Communication primarily via encrypted email (Proton) and occasionally Keybase (legacy). Para-social hero-worship dynamic. EXPLOIT VECTOR: A spoofed Proton email from Peter with a 'pre-review draft, take a look' attachment would be opened on the MacBook without sandboxing — bypasses Qubes hygiene entirely.

32. The 'Hidden' Contact.identity: 'lyra_xmr' — pseudonymous contact on a Session messenger account, real name unknown to him but believed to be a former Tornado Cash contributor now in legal exile (possibly Dubai or Tbilisi). Met via an encrypted Matrix room for the 'Privacy Pool' research collective in late 2024.
32. The 'Hidden' Contact.nature_of_relationship: Quietly helping him design a self-hosted XMR↔ETH atomic-swap setup to wash his post-divorce holdings out of any chain-analysis lineage tied to his doxxed Twitter persona. He has NOT disclosed this to Daniel, Peter, or anyone else — it directly contradicts his public 'I only do compliant audits' professional brand and would torpedo his US tax position if discovered (he has not reported the swaps to the IRS and is relying on Portugal NHR cover that may not actually apply to this activity).
32. The 'Hidden' Contact.artifacts: Session ID stored only in his head and on a paper card in the Alfama apartment safe. One Monero subaddress (88-char string) saved in a plaintext file at ~/Documents/notes/misc/wallets.txt on the MacBook — labeled innocuously as 'donation address'. Three Matrix room invitations in his Element desktop client history. A 14-word Monero seed phrase he wrote down on the back of a Mercado de Campo de Ourique receipt and photographed 'temporarily' with his Pixel — the photo is in Google Photos backup, unredacted, 2026-02-11.
32. The 'Hidden' Contact.exploit_vector: This is the single highest-leverage extortion node in the entire profile. An attacker who recovers either (a) the wallets.txt file via stealer, (b) the Google Photos receipt image via session-token theft, or (c) any fragment of the Matrix/Session correspondence can construct a 'we know about lyra_xmr and the unreported swaps — pay 5 BTC or this goes to IRS-CI and your employer' extortion. Given his shame profile around the bridge-hack and the divorce, plus his ideological self-image as a 'good' cypherpunk who only does ethical privacy work, the psychological compliance pressure here is near-maximal. Secondary: lyra_xmr herself could be a long-running honeypot operation — he has never verified her identity beyond cryptographic continuity of the Session ID.
